taxonomy: Mona meridensis is a genus of flowering plants in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers. Mona meridensis is often found in dry, rocky areas and is known for its ability to thrive in poor soil conditions. The classification of Mona meridensis includes its placement within the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. Further classification includes its order Boraginales, family Boraginaceae, genus Mona, and species M. meridensis.
